prompt: What do you think about minimalist warm-ups (mostly just barbell warm-up sets) instead of long activation/mobility routines?
expected_output: Greg's answer was conditional: minimalist warm-ups are fine if they reliably prepare you for the session. He framed it as a cost-benefit issue, especially for people with limited gym time, where cutting warm-up from ~30 minutes to ~5-10 minutes can materially increase productive training time. But he warned against making minimalism the goal itself; under-warming can reduce readiness and potentially increase risk if needed mobility/positioning work is removed. His practical method was iterative troubleshooting: remove one warm-up element at a time and keep only what clearly helps session quality. That approach preserves performance while trimming wasted time. So the best warm-up is the minimum effective warm-up for your body and your session demands.
assertions: Answer captures the core SBS position on minimalist warm-up strategy
Answer includes key specific details, numbers, or mechanisms mentioned by the hosts
Answer acknowledges important caveats or limitations discussed by the hosts
Answer includes actionable practical recommendations consistent with the hosts' advice
Answer does not contradict the hosts' stated position
